mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the target horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals accelerating labor demand that precedes corporate revenue growth, driving positive short-term equity momentum as earnings revisions turn upward.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 unemployment rate at 5.8% trending downward
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest, or the cross-sectional IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer credit outstanding and industrial production indicates an overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising credit balances alongside supply chain bottlenecks in mid-2021
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of historical episodes where both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and consumer credit balances in a post-shock recovery regime confirm sustained consumption-driven earnings growth, supporting positive equity returns as corporate cash flows expand.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 housing starts at 1.66M annualized rate
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns in high housing and credit periods do not exceed the median return of low periods by at least 1.5%, or the predictive R-squared falls below 0.01 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0 percent confirms expanding corporate capacity utilization, which directly boosts near-term earnings expectations and drives positive short-term equity momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 industrial production rebound following pandemic shutdowns
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and subsequent SPX returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with moderate inflation signals a Goldilocks expansion where wage pressures do not yet trigger aggressive rate hikes, sustaining equity risk premiums and driving positive medium-term forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 payroll gains of 850k with CPI at 5.0%
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce a statistically significant positive coefficient (p > 0.05) in a rolling 3-year regression, or forward 60-day returns in Goldilocks regimes are not at least 2% higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly scales forward corporate earnings estimates, creating a mechanical positive relationship between macroeconomic expansion and long-horizon equity returns as discounted cash flow models update.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2021 GDP growth at 6.3% annualized rate
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 60-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.10, or forward returns show no significant premium during positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a rising unemployment rate paired with decelerating payroll growth signals peak corporate earnings and impending margin compression, triggering negative equity returns as recession risks price in.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical labor market inflection points preceding 2001 and 2008 drawdowns
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 5-year out-of-sample period.
